workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Just got this from Andrew Taylor at Pacific Northwest Outfitters -- I need help figuring out partner engagement. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 14:51 EST
Subject: Follow-up: partner engagement Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with partner engagement. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 16:25 EST
Subject: Follow-up: partner engagement Review

Our team encountered some challenges with partner engagement. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 09:14 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Pacific Northwest Outfitters partner engagement

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with partner engagement. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Pacific Northwest Outfitters: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-O trap (MSX delta requires approval for obvious updates (next-step changes)); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-O failure mode (MSX delta requires approval for obvious updates (next-step changes))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-O -- MSX delta requires approval for obvious updates (next-step changes)
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
